meaning: adj.无人要买的,卖不掉的,不能卖的
examples: This could help jump-start the market in these currently unsellable assets.
It is a similar story in Mumbai, where flats are unsellable and property developers are beginning to go bust.
Financial firms we now know directed vast quantities of capital into the construction of unsellable houses and empty shopping malls.
If the firm that made the car has gone bust, it becomes virtually unsellable secondhand.
But even if the idea proves politically unsellable, says Mr Walkden, it is a useful corrective.
